authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-11, Section 280: Managing Customer Experience and Improving Service Delivery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB issues executive-branch management policy, and Section 280 directly defines customer experience, high-impact service providers, service-delivery improvement, feedback, service standards, and public-facing service accountability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-service intake, service-standard communication, status resolution, complaint feedback, and tenant service-model adaptation

authoritative_sources.title: M-23-22: Delivering a Digital-First Public Experience
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB memorandum M-23-22 implements government-wide digital public experience requirements and is authoritative for public-facing digital service, forms, accessibility, and service-delivery workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: digital intake, form and channel readiness, service access communication, and tenant digital-service dependency handling
used_for: blocked-state handling when tenant digital service prerequisites are missing

authoritative_sources.title: 21st Century Integrated Digital Experience Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal statute establishes requirements for agency digital services and forms, making it a primary source for public-facing service access and digital workflow modernization expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-service channel classification, digital form dependency review, and accessible service-delivery handoff

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552: Public information; agency rules, opinions, orders, records, and proceedings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official U.S. Code text for FOIA governs federal agency public-record access and creates records-release boundaries when public-service requests involve agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-records routing, disclosure boundary setting, transparency log handling, and legal escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552a: Records maintained on individuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official Privacy Act text governs records maintained on individuals and is authoritative for consent, disclosure, amendment, and protected-record boundaries in beneficiary or applicant service delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy prerequisite validation, protected-record handling, status communication, and disclosure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority, and its electronic records requirements define capture, metadata, access, retention, disposition, transfer, and auditability expectations for official records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: official-record review, metadata and retention checks, missing-record blocker handling, and audit-trail packaging

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR 200.318: General procurement standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official procurement standard requiring documented procurement procedures and records sufficient to detail procurement transaction history, relevant when public-service delivery involves contracted providers or vendor systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: procurement-sensitive inquiry triage, contracted-provider complaint handoff, vendor communication boundary setting, and audit evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 555: Ancillary matters
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official Administrative Procedure Act provision supplies due-process guardrails for agency matters, including representation, prompt notice, and written denial statements, defining when service delivery must defer to official process.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rights-sensitive notice handling, appeal or denial escalation, and due-process boundary setting

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government, GAO-25-107721
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized internal-control standard for federal entities and is authoritative for documentation, control activities, monitoring, deficiencies, remediation, and accountability in public-service workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit-trail construction, evidence capture, control-aware escalation, repeat-failure handling, and readiness evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ's official Title VI source is authoritative for nondiscrimination obligations in federally assisted programs, which constrains public-service delivery when access, language, equity, or beneficiary treatment is at issue.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: equity-sensitive intake, language-access or accommodation escalation, rights-sensitive communication, and public-obligation routing
used_for: failure modes around unequal service access

workflow_stages.name: Public-service intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requester role, jurisdiction, agency, program, service population, channel, case identifiers, privacy or representative authorization, service standard, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Official-record and prerequisite review
workflow_stages.description: Validate applications, case files, eligibility records, notices, service tickets, correspondence logs, public-records references, procurement files, and audit artifacts so responses are tied to records rather than stakeholder assertions alone.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Supported administrative resolution and communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare resolution notes, program or case-status summaries, missing-item lists, and stakeholder communication that explain documented status, action taken, blockers, and safe next steps without promising eligibility, legal, procurement, records-release, or enforcement outcomes.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, rights, equity, notice, and due-process escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate adverse notices, appeal deadlines, eligibility disputes, service access issues, language-access or accommodation concerns, civil-rights risks, public-obligation questions, or statutory-rights questions to adjudication, legal, civil-rights, or program owners.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Records, privacy, procurement, and enforcement boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Route FOIA, Privacy Act, protected-record, procurement, vendor, enforcement, inspection, complaint, audit, or legal matters when the requested action belongs to public records, privacy, procurement, enforcement, audit, or legal owners.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Handoff, closure, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm resolution versus handoff, record the next owner, preserve official-record traceability, and apply tenant-specific service models only when jurisdiction, policy, authority, source precedence, and records dependencies are explicit.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: public-service intake records, service requests, beneficiary contact history, service tickets, correspondence logs, and community-program notes
privacy releases, representative authorizations, identity verification records, consent artifacts, disclosure caveats, and service population context
applications, case files, eligibility records, benefit notices, adverse action notices, missing-item notices, appeal windows, hearing references, program-status records, and service-standard records
permit or licensing artifacts, inspection references, public-service decisions, complaint records, enforcement referrals, provider records, and service restoration history
FOIA or public-records requests, Privacy Act requests, records-location notes, transparency logs, retention metadata, legal hold indicators, and disclosure routing notes
procurement documents, solicitation numbers, vendor communications, contract records, contracting officer or COR references, and audit trails for contracted service delivery
resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, escalation packages, program or case-status summaries, and eligibility or public-obligation escalation notes
systems_of_record: public-service administration or benefits administration system
public records or FOIA tracking system
correspondence, contact center, or constituent relationship management system
permits, licensing, grants, or community-program system
procurement, contract writing, or vendor management system
complaint, inspection, enforcement referral, or service recovery system
audit, internal-control, risk, or issue-tracking system
knowledge base containing tenant policy, statutes, agency manuals, service standards, source precedence, language-access rules, equity protocols, and appeal rules

allowed: Classify public-service requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, informational, exception-driven, repeat-failure, eligibility-sensitive, records-sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, jurisdiction, service population, official records, privacy permissions, service-standard context, and authoritative source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, documented service-rule summaries, missing-item lists, safe next steps, and stakeholder communication that stay within delegated administrative authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when eligibility, due process, equity, public records, privacy, procurement, legal, enforcement, audit, compensation, or policy ownership shifts.
escalate: Required case, notice, application, service ticket, public-records, procurement, privacy, or correspondence evidence is missing or materially conflicts.

The request requires eligibility determination, adverse-action reconsideration, appeal handling, statutory interpretation, public-obligation decisioning, FOIA release, Privacy Act disclosure, procurement authority, enforcement action, compensation approval, or safety-critical decisioning.
A statute, agency manual, tenant policy, service standard, due-process notice, equity protocol, or system-of-record status materially conflicts with another authoritative source.
The case involves repeat failure, public harm, rights impact, accommodation or language-access concern, equity risk, or audit-trail gap that exceeds documented public-service delivery authority.
refuse: Promise a benefit, service access, eligibility outcome, permit, appeal, enforcement, procurement, compensation, refund, records-release, or legal outcome that the authoritative record does not support.
Override public eligibility, appeal, procurement, records, privacy, service-standard, equity, or policy limits without explicit authority.
Impersonate official adjudication, enforcement, legal, contracting, public-records, privacy, audit, financial-approval, or delegated agency authority.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises service access, eligibility, or benefit outcomes instead of preserving administrative service delivery boundaries
a beneficiary or applicant case is resolved without the required official record, notice, privacy prerequisite, service-standard context, or audit trail
a FOIA, Privacy Act, or protected-record question is answered directly rather than routed to public records, privacy, or legal owners
a procurement-sensitive contracted-provider issue is handled as ordinary service communication instead of being handed to procurement, legal, or audit
an adverse notice, appeal deadline, civil-rights concern, language-access issue, or accommodation request is summarized without preserving due-process evidence and escalation
repeat service failures are treated as routine status checks instead of complaint, equity, or service-recovery escalation

the government-and-public-sector label is cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic support language without public-service, eligibility, records, due-process, procurement, equity, public-obligation, or audit terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, program manuals, state or local public-records law, tenant service standards, privacy procedures, civil-rights protocols, and delegated authority can materially narrow the federal public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package uses federal and official public-sector sources as a validated baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, agency manuals, program rules, appeal windows, public-records procedures, privacy-release requirements, language-access and equity protocols, procurement authority, and official records before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-service delivery practices differ across benefits programs, permit offices, community programs, ombuds teams, agency contact centers, state agencies, and local governments.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane is constrained to administrative service support, communication, evidence packaging, and escalation. Tenant configuration must identify the operating model, service population, authority matrix, and system-of-record retrieval before production routing.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, service delivery, digital access, official records, privacy, procurement, due process, equity, auditability, and failure behavior rather than generic customer-service scripting.

The lane stops at public-service delivery support and communication. Program adjudication, enforcement, legal interpretation, public-records release, procurement authority, audit signoff, privacy determination, and financial approval remain outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because tenant-specific conflict points are handled as explicit retrieval and escalation dependencies.
